Today's Matches
🏆 Round of 16 almost done — Spain, Belgium, Morocco, France, Norway and England are into the quarter-finals; only Argentina–Egypt and Switzerland–Colombia remain, both today (Tue July 7)◆🇪🇸 Spain edge Portugal — substitute Mikel Merino’s stoppage-time strike settles a tense all-Iberian tie 1–0 in Dallas, sending De la Fuente’s side into the last eight without conceding all tournament◆🇵🇹 End of an era — at 41, Cristiano Ronaldo’s final World Cup ends in defeat as Portugal fall to Spain; the first man to score at six World Cups bows out in the Round of 16◆🇧🇪 Belgium dismantle the USA — a Charles De Ketelaere brace, Vanaken and a late Lukaku strike sink the co-hosts 4–1 in Seattle; the Red Devils reach a third straight quarter-final◆🇺🇸 Hosts out — Malik Tillman’s free-kick briefly levelled, but the USMNT’s home run ends in the last 16 once more; Pochettino’s three wins are still a record for a US men’s coach◆🇦🇷 Today (TRT) — Argentina v Egypt in Atlanta (Tue 19:00): Messi’s holders, seven goals to his name, face a Salah-led Egypt in their first-ever Round of 16◆🇨🇭 Also today — Switzerland v Colombia in Vancouver (Tue 23:00 TRT) closes the Round of 16; the unbeaten Swiss meet Los Cafeteros for the last quarter-final place◆🏊 Quarter-finals taking shape — Spain v Belgium (Los Angeles) and Norway v England (Miami, July 11) join Morocco v France (Boston, July 9); the fourth QF awaits tonight’s winners◆⚽ Golden Boot — Messi, Mbappé and Haaland stay locked together on seven goals as the last eight nears; Kane leads the chasing pack on six◆👋 Farewells — Portugal and the co-hosts USA join Brazil, Mexico, Canada and Paraguay on the way out; six of the original 16 last-16 sides have now fallen◆🏆 Road to the final — from the quarter-finals it is three wins to the trophy, with the 2026 World Cup final set for MetLife Stadium, New Jersey on July 19◆🏆 Round of 16 almost done — Spain, Belgium, Morocco, France, Norway and England are into the quarter-finals; only Argentina–Egypt and Switzerland–Colombia remain, both today (Tue July 7)◆🇪🇸 Spain edge Portugal — substitute Mikel Merino’s stoppage-time strike settles a tense all-Iberian tie 1–0 in Dallas, sending De la Fuente’s side into the last eight without conceding all tournament◆🇵🇹 End of an era — at 41, Cristiano Ronaldo’s final World Cup ends in defeat as Portugal fall to Spain;     Player of the Round
    👑 Erling Haaland
    🇳🇴 Norway

    Two late goals — a towering header and a drilled finish — knocked out five-time winners Brazil and carried Norway to a first-ever World Cup quarter-final. Now level on seven with Messi and Mbappé atop the Golden Boot.

    2Goals
    7Tourn. goals
    1stNorway QF
    Selector’s Notes

    Front three of R16 drama: captain Haaland (brace to dump out Brazil), Mbappé’s decisive penalty past Paraguay, and De Ketelaere’s two goals and an assist in Belgium’s 4–1 rout of the USA.

    Engine room: Ounahi (two goals v Canada), Ødegaard driving Norway, and Brahim Díaz (two assists, an African World Cup record). Behind them Hakimi & Aguerd anchored Morocco’s shutout, Ajer subdued Brazil, Konsa marshalled England’s ten men, and Nyland saved a penalty.

    On the bench 🇬🇧 Bellingham 🇪🇸 Merino 🇬🇧 Kane 🇧🇪 Lukaku
    Tournament Overview

    Executive Summary

    48
    Teams · 3 hosts
    🇫🇷
    Model leader
    10
    Left · R16 ends
    6
    Into the QF

    First-ever 48-team World Cup, across USA, Canada and Mexico. The new Round of 32 shields the big sides but opens a knockout path for outsiders.

    Europe on top. France lead the model with Spain and England close behind, all three into the quarter-finals. But the knockouts have bitten: Germany and Netherlands fell on penalties in the R32, and Ronaldo's Portugal are out, beaten 1–0 by Spain in the last 16.

    Disruptors are real. Haaland's Norway stunned five-time winners Brazil 2–1 to reach a first-ever quarter-final, and Belgium put four past co-hosts USA to make the last eight. Morocco knocked out the Dutch and swept Canada aside to reach back-to-back quarter-finals. Brazil's exit is their earliest since 1990.

    Argentina lean on Messi. He passed Klose, hit a record 20th WC goal, and shares the Golden Boot lead on seven with Mbappé and Haaland — but needed 111 mins past Cape Verde. Durability over a seven-game run is the question, with Egypt next in the R16.

    R16 all but done. Spain, Belgium, Morocco, France, Norway and England are through. Merino’s late goal ended Ronaldo’s Portugal 1–0, and Belgium routed the co-hosts USA 4–1. Morocco–France (Boston, July 9), Norway–England (Miami, July 11) and Spain–Belgium (Los Angeles) are set. Two ties remain, both today (Tuesday July 7): Argentina–Egypt and Switzerland–Colombia.

    Section 03
    Simulation Engine

    Simulation

    Model projects the whole tournament forward — every unplayed match, group tables, bracket, champion. Current keeps played results, sims the rest; Before the Tournament re-sims everything from pre-tournament ratings.

    Model predictions, not official results. Poisson goal model + power ratings, form and home edge; probabilities from a Monte Carlo run. Real results (through July 6) never overwritten. Hit Regenerate any time.
    ⚽ Poisson goals 📊 Power ratings 🔥 Current form 🏟️ Home edge 🎲 Monte Carlo

    🎮 How to Play

    Goal: outscore the CPU over two 60-sec halves. Most goals wins.

    Desktop: W A S D / ↑ ↓ ← → move · Space or run into ball to strike.

    Mobile: drag joystick to move — ball kicks on contact. Landscape works best.

    📊 Difficulty by Nation

    How hard each country plays — your level, their strength, and your own team's strength combined. Pick your team first to personalise it.

    Section 09
    Classification System

    Tier Breakdown

    Tier 1 — Title Favorites
    6teams
    🇫🇷 France🇪🇸 Spain🇦🇷 Argentina🏴󠁧󠁢󠁥󠁮󠁧󠁿 England🇵🇹 Portugal🇧🇷 Brazil
    4/6 into the QFBrazil & Portugal out
    Real path to the trophy — depth, pedigree, seven-game staying power. France, Spain and England are into the quarter-finals; Brazil fell to Norway and Portugal to Spain, ending Ronaldo's final World Cup. Four of the six remain.
    Tier 2 — Contenders
    7teams
    🇳🇴 Norway🇲🇽 Mexico🇨🇴 Colombia🇧🇪 Belgium🇲🇦 Morocco🇨🇭 Switzerland🇺🇸 USA
    Norway, Morocco & Belgium in QFMexico & USA out
    Can reach a final with the right variance. Norway (past Brazil), Morocco (past Canada) and Belgium (4–1 over the USA) are into the quarter-finals; co-hosts Mexico and the USA are out. Colombia and Switzerland play the last R16 tie between them.
    Tier 3 — Dark Horses
    9teams
    🇨🇦 Canada🇵🇾 Paraguay🇪🇬 Egypt🇦🇺 Australia🇩🇪 Germany🇳🇱 Netherlands🇯🇵 Japan🇬🇭 Ghana🇨🇻 Cape Verde
    Egypt alive8 out
    Egypt are the last of this group standing, into the last 16 for the first time; Canada and Paraguay bowed out in R16, above the fallen giants they leapfrogged: Germany, Netherlands, Japan.
    Tier 4 — Knockout Threats
    10teams
    🇸🇳 Senegal🇭🇷 Croatia🇦🇹 Austria🇨🇮 Ivory Coast🇨🇩 DR Congo🇿🇦 South Africa🇸🇪 Sweden🇪🇨 Ecuador🇩🇿 Algeria🇧🇦 Bosnia & Herzegovina
    All out in R32
    Ten sides who won a knockout berth from a 48-team field, then fell in R32. Senegal, Croatia and DR Congo went closest — threat, even in heartbreak.
    Tier 5 — Long Shots
    16teams
    🇰🇷 South Korea🇹🇷 Türkiye🇺🇾 Uruguay🏴󠁧󠁢󠁳󠁣󠁴󠁿 Scotland🇮🇷 Iran🇨🇿 Czechia🇹🇳 Tunisia🇸🇦 Saudi Arabia🇳🇿 New Zealand🇶🇦 Qatar🇺🇿 Uzbekistan🇵🇦 Panama🇮🇶 Iraq🇯🇴 Jordan🇭🇹 Haiti🇨🇼 Curaçao
    Out in groups
    Sixteen sides out at the group stage. Uruguay the heaviest fall — no win; debutants Curaçao leave with a historic first World Cup point.
    Tier 1 — Title Favorites
    7teams
    🇫🇷 France🇪🇸 Spain🇦🇷 Argentina🏴󠁧󠁢󠁥󠁮󠁧󠁿 England🇵🇹 Portugal🇩🇪 Germany🇧🇷 Brazil
    Ratings 90–96
    Seven sides with the depth and pedigree to win it. France top the model at 96; Germany still firmly in the mix pre-tournament.
    Tier 2 — Contenders
    9teams
    🇳🇴 Norway🇳🇱 Netherlands🇨🇴 Colombia🇧🇪 Belgium🇯🇵 Japan🇲🇦 Morocco🇲🇽 Mexico🇨🇭 Switzerland🇸🇳 Senegal
    Ratings 66–77
    Can reach a final with the right draw. Haaland, Gakpo and 2022 semifinalists Morocco headline the pre-tournament dark-horse pack.
    Tier 3 — Dark Horses
    8teams
    🇪🇨 Ecuador🇺🇾 Uruguay🇺🇸 USA🇰🇷 South Korea🇨🇦 Canada🇦🇹 Austria🇹🇷 Türkiye🇨🇮 Ivory Coast
    Ratings 58–65
    Well-organised sides that could spring a R16 surprise. Co-hosts USA and Canada, plus Bielsa’s Uruguay, lead this group.
    Tier 4 — Knockout Threats
    8teams
    🇭🇷 Croatia🇪🇬 Egypt🏴󠁧󠁢󠁳󠁣󠁴󠁿 Scotland🇩🇿 Algeria🇮🇷 Iran🇵🇾 Paraguay🇦🇺 Australia🇸🇪 Sweden
    Ratings 51–58
    Battling, well-drilled teams who could nick a knockout berth. 2018 finalists Croatia and a Salah-led Egypt headline.
    Tier 5 — Long Shots
    16teams
    🇬🇭 Ghana🇧🇦 Bosnia & Herzegovina🇨🇿 Czechia🇿🇦 South Africa🇨🇩 DR Congo🇹🇳 Tunisia🇸🇦 Saudi Arabia🇳🇿 New Zealand🇶🇦 Qatar🇺🇿 Uzbekistan🇵🇦 Panama🇮🇶 Iraq🇨🇻 Cape Verde🇯🇴 Jordan🇭🇹 Haiti🇨🇼 Curaçao
    Ratings ≤50
    Sixteen sides expected to exit in the groups — debutants Cape Verde, Curaçao and Jordan chasing history against the odds.
    Section 08
    Value Intelligence

    Dark Horse Report

    Seven teams most likely to outperform their odds — evidence over narrative.

    🇳🇴 Norway
    Contender
    #7PWR 77QF ✓
    The story of the round: Haaland’s late double knocked out five-time winners Brazil 2–1 to send Norway to a first-ever World Cup quarter-final. England await in Miami.
    ⭐ Erling Haaland
    ↑ Into the quarter-finals
    🇵🇾 Paraguay
    Dark Horse
    #15PWR 61OUT · R16
    A run to remember: stunned Germany on penalties, then held France goalless for 70 minutes before a Mbappé penalty settled a 1–0 defeat in Philadelphia. Out with heads high.
    ⭐ Orlando Gill (GK)
    ↓ Eliminated by France
    🇲🇦 Morocco
    Contender
    #11PWR 68R16 ✓
    2022’s semifinalists knocked out the Netherlands on penalties, then swept co-hosts Canada 3–0 — Ounahi’s double the difference. Back-to-back quarter-finals; France next in Boston.
    ⭐ Achraf Hakimi
    ↑ Into the quarter-finals
    🇨🇴 Colombia
    Contender
    #9PWR 75R16 ✓
    Group K winners, unbeaten and conceding once, then a controlled 1–0 of Ghana. Cohesive, resilient and dangerous in transition — Switzerland next.
    ⭐ Luis Díaz
    ↑ QF upset specialist
    🇺🇸 USA
    Contender
    #13PWR 66OUT · R16
    The co-hosts' home run ended in the last 16, beaten 4–1 by Belgium in Seattle. Three group wins were a US-record haul, but the step up proved too steep.
    ⭐ Malik Tillman
    ↓ Out in the Round of 16
    🇨🇭 Switzerland
    Contender
    #12PWR 67R16 ✓
    Beat Algeria 2–0 for a first World Cup knockout win since 1938. Efficient, well-drilled and tournament-hardened — the Colombia tie awaits.
    ⭐ Johan Manzambi
    ↑ Reliable knockout threat
    🇪🇬 Egypt
    Dark Horse
    #16PWR 58R16 ✓
    Into the last 16 for the first time, edging Australia 4–2 on penalties. A fit Salah gives them a puncher’s chance — but holders Argentina stand in the way in Atlanta.
    ⭐ Mohamed Salah
    ↑ Salah the wildcard
    🇳🇱 Netherlands
    Dark Horse
    #9PWR 77
    Attacking flair with Gakpo and real depth up top. Tournament pedigree and a settled core make a deep run credible.
    ⭐ Cody Gakpo
    ↑ Semifinal ceiling
    🇺🇾 Uruguay
    Dark Horse
    #18PWR 63
    Two-time champions with defensive steel and forward talent under Bielsa — a classic tournament over-performer.
    ⭐ Federico Valverde
    ↑ QF pedigree
    🇨🇴 Colombia
    Contender
    #10PWR 72
    Cohesive, resilient and dangerous in transition, with one of the loudest fanbases. Suited to North American heat.
    ⭐ Luis Díaz
    ↑ QF upset specialist
    🇸🇳 Senegal
    Knockout Threat
    #16PWR 66
    African heavyweights with pace and power. Physical, direct and tipped to reach the knockouts.
    ⭐ Nicolas Jackson
    ↑ R16 threat
    🇪🇨 Ecuador
    Dark Horse
    #17PWR 65
    Young, well-organised side capable of springing a surprise on a bigger name in a one-off.
    ⭐ Moisés Caicedo
    ↑ Surprise package
    🇭🇷 Croatia
    Knockout Threat
    #25PWR 58
    2018 finalists still marshalled by the evergreen Modrić — big-game know-how that shows up in knockouts.
    ⭐ Luka Modrić
    ↑ Deep-run experience
    🇺🇸 USA
    Dark Horse
    #19PWR 59
    Co-hosts with a talented young core and home support — conditions and crowd can carry them past their rating.
    ⭐ Christian Pulisic
    ↑ Home advantage real
    Section 10
    Critical Concerns

    Questions — Top 10 Teams

    For each top-10 team, the single concern most capable of preventing a title run.

    #1
    🇫🇷 France
    Power Score 94
    Can they keep Mbappé fit for 7 games?
    ● Mbappé fitness
    Perfect Group I, model favourite, now into the quarter-finals after seeing off Paraguay. Whole ceiling still rides on Mbappé's body — Morocco next in Boston.
    ⚠️ Kylian Mbappé
    #2
    🇪🇸 Spain
    Power Score 92
    Is Yamal fully match-ready?
    ● Yamal durability
    Five games, no goals conceded; Merino's late strike saw off Portugal. With Nico Williams out, attack leans hard on a managed Yamal — Belgium next in the QF.
    ⚠️ Lamine Yamal
    #3
    🇦🇷 Argentina
    Power Score 90
    Can 39-yr Messi last 7 games?
    ● Messi legs
    Won Group J, Messi now all-time top scorer. Needed 111 mins past Cape Verde — fatigue is the risk. Egypt next.
    ⚠️ Lionel Messi
    #4
    🏴󠁧󠁢󠁥󠁮󠁧󠁿 England
    Power Score 89
    Will the pressure culture repeat?
    ● Big-game nerves
    Kane their WC top scorer; survived the Azteca with ten men to beat Mexico 3–2. 60-year drought, James out, Norway in the Miami quarter-final next.
    ⚠️ Reece James (out)
    #5
    🇧🇪 Belgium
    Power Score 76
    Peaking at the right time?
    ● Defensive lapses
    Unbeaten in 18; De Ketelaere's brace crushed the USA 4–1. The front line clicks, but Spain's clean-sheet defence is a sterner QF test.
    ⚠️ Charles De Ketelaere
    #6
    🇲🇦 Morocco
    Power Score 68
    Can they finish the chances they create?
    ● Cutting edge
    Beat the Dutch on penalties and Canada 3–0 to reach back-to-back quarter-finals, but were second best for a half. France next in Boston.
    ⚠️ Achraf Hakimi
    #7
    🇳🇴 Norway
    Power Score 77
    Can they handle a second heavyweight?
    ● Zero pedigree
    Haaland's late double stunned Brazil to reach a first-ever quarter-final. Pedigree still thin, but the ceiling is obvious — England next in Miami.
    ⚠️ Erling Haaland
    #8
    🇨🇭 Switzerland
    Power Score 67
    Enough goals to go deep?
    ● Cutting edge
    Beat Algeria 2–0 for a first knockout win since 1938 — efficient and well-drilled, but reliant on Manzambi's spark. Colombia next in Vancouver.
    ⚠️ Johan Manzambi
    #9
    🇨🇴 Colombia
    Power Score 75
    Who scores when Díaz is contained?
    ● Díaz dependency
    Group K winners; Díaz muted twice. Arias beat Ghana without him — good sign. Switzerland a sterner test.
    ⚠️ Luis Díaz
    #10
    🇪🇬 Egypt
    Power Score 58
    Can Salah drag them through?
    ● Lone talisman
    Beat Australia on penalties for a first-ever last-16 place. Salah is the difference-maker, but holders Argentina are a huge step up.
    ⚠️ Mohamed Salah
    #1
    🇫🇷 France
    Power Score 96
    Can Mbappé carry the favourites' tag?
    ● Weight of expectation
    World No.1, frightening depth. As top pick, pressure and Mbappé's fitness are the only real doubts.
    ⚠️ Kylian Mbappé
    #2
    🇪🇸 Spain
    Power Score 95
    Is the young core ready to win it all?
    ● Big-stage nerve
    Euro 2024 champs with Pedri, Rodri, Yamal. Elite control — question is closing out a WC so young.
    ⚠️ Lamine Yamal
    #3
    🇦🇷 Argentina
    Power Score 94
    How much is left in Messi's legs?
    ● Messi age
    Holders built round a 39-yr-old Messi. Ruthless core, but a deep run over 39 days is the ask.
    ⚠️ Lionel Messi
    #4
    🏴󠁧󠁢󠁥󠁮󠁧󠁿 England
    Power Score 93
    Can they finally handle the pressure?
    ● Tournament culture
    Deep, Tuchel-drilled, Kane + Bellingham. Talent never the issue — the mental block is.
    ⚠️ Jude Bellingham
    #5
    🇵🇹 Portugal
    Power Score 92
    Golden gen: does Ronaldo help or hurt?
    ● Ronaldo balance
    Pace and creativity everywhere. Dark-horse title pick if the team isn't built too far around CR7.
    ⚠️ Cristiano Ronaldo
    #6
    🇩🇪 Germany
    Power Score 91
    Can they shed the underachiever tag?
    ● Fragile defence
    Four-time champs, talented core. Question marks sit at the back and on knockout nerve.
    ⚠️ Antonio Rüdiger
    #7
    🇧🇷 Brazil
    Power Score 90
    Is Ancelotti's system ready in time?
    ● New system
    Individual quality unmatched, but Ancelotti joined late — cohesion under pressure untested.
    ⚠️ Vinícius Jr
    #8
    🇳🇴 Norway
    Power Score 77
    Zero pedigree — can Haaland deliver?
    ● No experience
    Most direct route to goal of any outsider. But tournament inexperience is a genuine unknown.
    ⚠️ Erling Haaland
    #9
    🇳🇱 Netherlands
    Power Score 77
    Enough steel behind the flair?
    ● Defensive depth
    Attacking talent with Gakpo and depth up top. Backline is the question in the latter stages.
    ⚠️ Cody Gakpo
    #10
    🇨🇴 Colombia
    Power Score 72
    Who scores when Díaz is contained?
    ● Díaz dependency
    Cohesive, dangerous in transition. Live outside bet — but heavy reliance on Luis Díaz.
    ⚠️ Luis Díaz
    Section 06
    Evidence-Based Forecasts

    Tournament Predictions

    Probability-weighted calls, not certainties — every one has a live alternative. Current reflects the tournament as it stands; Before the Tournament is the model’s pre-tournament forecast from the opening strength ratings.

    Most Likely Semifinalists
    🇫🇷 🇪🇸 🇦🇷 🏴󠁧󠁢󠁥󠁮󠁧󠁿
    France · Spain · Argentina · England
    France headline a cleared left half; Spain, Argentina and England crowd the right — Spain already saw off Portugal, so the survivors now collide early on that side.
    ConfidenceMedium
    Gatecrashers: Brazil · Portugal
    Most Likely Finalists
    🇫🇷 FRAVSARG 🇦🇷
    The bracket splits them perfectly — France left, Argentina right — so a 2022 rematch needs no early collision. Spain are the top threat to reshuffle it.
    ConfidenceMedium
    Alt final: France vs Spain
    🏆
    Predicted Champion
    🇫🇷 France
    Won Group I at +8 with a Dembélé hat-trick, and their half opened up when Germany fell. Squad depth over seven games edges Spain’s pure talent.
    Title odds27%
    Closest challenger: Spain
    Predicted Golden Ball
    🇫🇷
    Kylian MbappéFrance · Forward
    Title-winning runs almost always produce the Golden Ball — if France lift it, the tournament’s most dangerous attacker is the inevitable pick.
    Alt: Lamine Yamal (Spain)
    Predicted Golden Boot
    🇫🇷
    Kylian MbappéFrance · 7 goals
    🇦🇷Messi7
    🇫🇷Mbappé7
    🇳🇴Haaland7
    🏴󠁧󠁢󠁥󠁮󠁧󠁿Kane6
    Haaland’s brace to sink Brazil pulls him level with Messi and Mbappé on seven — a three-way tie, with Kane one back. Mbappé leads on the assist tiebreak and France’s projected run could yet decide it.
    Biggest Upset
    🇳🇴 Norwayknock out🏴󠁧󠁢󠁥󠁮󠁧󠁿 England
    Having already dumped out Brazil, Haaland (7 goals) and Norway’s direct, transition-heavy style meet England in the Miami quarter-final — a genuine chance to fell a second heavyweight.
    Upset likelihoodLive
    AN
    Analyst’s Note: Where We Differ From Consensus

    🇳🇴 #7 ▲Norway vindicated: form (8/8 qualifying wins, 37 goals) kept them high all along — and Haaland’s late double to knock out Brazil has carried them to a first-ever quarter-final against England.

    🇦🇷 #3Argentina hold third: a perfect Group J and elite squad quality earn the spot, but a survived 3–2 scare against Cape Verde sets up a brutal right-half gauntlet — Egypt next in the last 16.

    🇩🇪 #18 ▼12Germany’s fall — the price of a KO exit: an excellent squad topped Group E, then lost the shootout 4–3 to Paraguay. An eliminated side can’t hold a title-tier slot.

    🇧🇷 #6 ▼Brazil out — the system never clicked: a flat draw with Morocco and a stoppage-time escape past Japan flagged the doubts, and Norway confirmed them, winning 2–1 in the last 16. An eliminated side can no longer hold a title-tier ranking.

    🇪🇸 #2Spain a fraction behind France: won Group H without conceding, then dismantled Austria 3–0 as Simón broke the shutout record. On talent it’s a coin flip; France lead on depth.

    Most Likely Semifinalists
    🇫🇷 🇪🇸 🇦🇷 🇩🇪
    France · Spain · Argentina · Germany
    The four highest pre-tournament ratings. England, Portugal and Brazil sit just outside and are the likeliest to break into the last four.
    ConfidenceMedium
    Just outside: England · Brazil
    Most Likely Finalists
    🇫🇷 FRAVSESP 🇪🇸
    The model’s two strongest sides on paper — a 96-vs-95 rating gap that’s effectively a coin flip. Argentina are the third wheel most likely to intervene.
    ConfidenceLow
    Alt final: France vs Argentina
    🏆
    Predicted Champion
    🇫🇷 France
    Top of the pre-tournament model at 96 — World No. 1, frightening forward depth and a settled spine. The narrowest of edges over Spain.
    Title odds19%
    Closest challenger: Spain (17%)
    Predicted Golden Ball
    🇫🇷
    Kylian MbappéFrance · Forward
    The favourites’ talisman and the tournament’s most dangerous attacker — if France go all the way, the individual award tends to follow.
    Alt: Jude Bellingham (England)
    Predicted Golden Boot
    🇫🇷
    Kylian MbappéFrance · projected
    🇫🇷Mbappé★
    🏴󠁧󠁢󠁥󠁮󠁧󠁿Kane★
    🇳🇴Haaland★
    🇦🇷Messi★
    A pre-tournament projection: elite finishers on the deepest sides top the race. Mbappé edges it on France’s expected run and penalty duties.
    Biggest Upset
    🇲🇦 Moroccoreach theSemi-finals
    The 2022 semi-finalists carry the organised, resilient profile that troubles bigger names — the dark horse most capable of a deep run from outside the top tier.
    Upset likelihoodLive
    AN
    Analyst’s Note: Where We Differ From Consensus

    🇫🇷 #1France over Spain at the very top: both are elite, but France’s forward depth across seven games gives them the narrowest edge over the Euro 2024 champions.

    🇳🇴 #8Norway higher than most: consensus is cautious on their tournament inexperience; the model weights an 8/8 qualifying run and Haaland’s ceiling to keep them among the contenders.

    🇦🇷 #3Argentina third, above England & Portugal: the holders’ squad quality — even adjusted for age — edges the two European sides just behind them.

    🇩🇪 #6Germany firmly in the top tier: Wirtz, Kimmich and Rüdiger anchor an objectively excellent squad; the post-Euro-2024 pessimism understates them.

    🇧🇷 #7Brazil marginally below the Europeans: Ancelotti’s system is new and brand weight tends to overprice them; current evidence places them just outside the top six.

    Section 11
    Analytical Framework

    Evaluation Analysis

    Nine weighted factors make each team’s Power Score (0–100) and Win Probability. Weights come from 2010–2022 WC outcome data. Form now outweighs pre-tournament billing — every team carries a movement chip, eliminated sides show OUT.

    🎯 9 factors🧮 Totals 100%📈 Power Score 0–100🔀 Form re-ranked
    Current Squad Quality25%
    Recent International Form20%
    Depth & Bench Strength12%
    Manager / Coaching Quality10%
    Tournament Experience8%
    World / Elo Ranking10%
    Injury Concerns5%
    Group-Stage Performance5%
    Knockout Path (QF taking shape)5%
    World Cup 2026
    Analytical Power Rankings — Kickoff Edition, June 11, 2026. Methodology-driven, evidence-based team assessments for the 48-team expanded tournament hosted across the United States, Canada, and Mexico.
    48 Teams 104 Matches 3 Host Nations 16 Venues June 11 — July 19
    Tournament: June 11 — July 19, 2026 · Final at MetLife Stadium, East Rutherford, New Jersey Power Rankings · Analytical Report · June 2026
